devanagari: अथ त्रयोदशोऽध्यायः । श्रीनारद उवाच । कल्पस्त्वेवं परिव्रज्य देहमात्रावशेषितः । ग्रामैकरात्रविधिना निरपेक्षश्चरेन्महीम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: atha trayodaśo'dhyāyaḥśrīnārada uvācakalpastvevaṃ parivrajya dehamātrāvaśeṣitaḥgrāmaikarātravidhinā nirapekṣaścarenmahīm
translation:
Nārada continued:
If a person is thus capable and fit (physically and mentally), he should take to the life of a vagrant recluse. Owning nothing else except his body, he should observe the rule of staying one night in a village, and thus wander over the earth, being totally desireless.

devanagari: बिभृयाद्यद्यसौ वासः कौपीनाच्छादनं परम् । त्यक्तं न लिङ्गाद्दण्डादेरन्यत्किञ्चिदनापदि ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: bibhṛyādyadyasau vāsaḥ kaupīnācchādanaṃ paramtyaktaṃ na liṅgāddaṇḍāderanyatkiñcidanāpadi
translation: If at all he is to wear a piece of cloth, it should be a strip of cloth just sufficient to cover his private parts. Unless there is some serious danger, he should not take anything which has been renounced except the staff and other emblems (e.g. kamaṇḍalu-water-pot) characteristic of a saṃnyāsin.

devanagari: एक एव चरेद्भिक्षुरात्मारामोऽनपाश्रयः । सर्वभूतसुहृच्छान्तो नारायणपरायणः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: eka eva caredbhikṣurātmārāmo'napāśrayaḥsarvabhūtasuhṛcchānto nārāyaṇaparāyaṇaḥ
translation: Delighted in his own Self and seeking no shelter, he should wander all alone as a mendicant subsisting on alms. He should be friendly to all beings, serene and devoted exclusively to Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: पश्येदात्मन्यदो विश्वं परे सदसतोऽव्यये । आत्मानं च परं ब्रह्म सर्वत्र सदसन्मये ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: paśyedātmanyado viśvaṃ pare sadasato'vyayeātmānaṃ ca paraṃ brahma sarvatra sadasanmaye
translation: [1] He should visualize this universe in the immutable ātman (Self) who is beyond (and distinct from) cause and effect and should perceive the Self as the Supreme Brahman present everywhere in the universe, (a product) of causes and consequences.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā asserts the Viśiṣṭādvaita position: Paramātman is distinct from the Sentient (Cid) and the Non-Sentient (Acid). By nature he is free from modifications. He (the recluse) should look upon the universe as the inseparable body of Paramātman Who is the Inner Controller.

devanagari: सुप्तिप्रबोधयोः सन्धावात्मनो गतिमात्मदृक् । पश्यन्बन्धं च मोक्षं च मायामात्रं न वस्तुतः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: suptiprabodhayoḥ sandhāvātmano gatimātmadṛkpaśyanbandhaṃ ca mokṣaṃ ca māyāmātraṃ na vastutaḥ
translation: During the mediate state between sleep and wakefulness, one should concentrate on the soul within, and should try to perceive the true nature of the Self. He should look upon bondage and emancipation as merely illusory and not real.

devanagari: नाभिनन्देद्ध्रुवं मृत्युमध्रुवं वास्य जीवितम् । कालं परं प्रतीक्षेत भूतानां प्रभवाप्ययम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nābhinandeddhruvaṃ mṛtyumadhruvaṃ vāsya jīvitamkālaṃ paraṃ pratīkṣeta bhūtānāṃ prabhavāpyayam
translation: He should welcome neither death, which is inevitable, nor life that is transitory. But he should simply wait for the Time which brings about the birth and death of beings (and that of one’s body as well.

devanagari: नासच्छास्त्रेषु सज्जेत नोपजीवेत जीविकाम् । वादवादांस्त्यजेत्तर्कान्पक्षं कंच न संश्रयेत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: nāsacchāstreṣu sajjeta nopajīveta jīvikāmvādavādāṃstyajettarkānpakṣaṃ kaṃca na saṃśrayet
translation: He should not be attached to Śāstras dealing with topics other than ātman. Nor should he try to maintain himself by following any profession (like astrology, medicine practised by him as a gṛhastha). He should avoid all form of disputation. He should not persistently adhere to any party.

devanagari: न शिष्याननुबध्नीत ग्रन्थान्नैवाभ्यसेद्बहून् । न व्याख्यामुपयुञ्जीत नारम्भानारभेत्क्वचित् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: na śiṣyānanubadhnīta granthānnaivābhyasedbahūnna vyākhyāmupayuñjīta nārambhānārabhetkvacit
translation: He should not attract students. Nor should he study numerous books[2] (lest they should cause confusion or distraction). He should not undertake the exposition of some sacred text[3]. Nor should he take up the establishment of any institution or monastery.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Books not dealing with Vedānta.

Footnote [3]:
Texts other than those on Vedānta—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa

devanagari: न यतेराश्रमः प्रायो धर्महेतुर्महात्मनः । शान्तस्य समचित्तस्य बिभृयादुत वा त्यजेत् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: na yaterāśramaḥ prāyo dharmaheturmahātmanaḥśāntasya samacittasya bibhṛyāduta vā tyajet
translation: The duties etc. prescribed for the order of recluses (saṃnyāsins) are not meant for securing religious merit in the case of the noble-souled ones (Paramahaṃsas) who are of serene and equable minds (The rules are to be observed till the recluse realizes the highest wisdom). Thereafter he may continue to practise the rules (for setting up an ideal life before the public) or discard them.

devanagari: अव्यक्तलिङ्गो व्यक्तार्थो मनीष्युन्मत्तबालवत् । कविर्मूकवदात्मानं स दृष्ट्या दर्शयेन्नृणाम् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: avyaktaliṅgo vyaktārtho manīṣyunmattabālavatkavirmūkavadātmānaṃ sa dṛṣṭyā darśayennṛṇām
translation: One whose external signs of being a recluse are not evident but continues his purpose (i.e. concentration with the Self), may, though learned, show himself (i.e. behave externally like) a mad person or an ignorant child; though highly intelligent and wise, he should appear to be dumb in the eyes of the public.

devanagari: तं शयानं धरोपस्थे कावेर्यां सह्यसानुनि । रजस्वलैस्तनूदेशैर्निगूढामलतेजसम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ śayānaṃ dharopasthe kāveryāṃ sahyasānunirajasvalaistanūdeśairnigūḍhāmalatejasam
translation: While Prahlāda, the beloved (devotee) of the Lord, accompanied by a few ministers, was touring through various worlds with a view to acquaint himself with the true nature of the people, he happened to see on the top of the Sahyādri (Western ghats) on the bank of the Kāverī, some person lying on bare ground, his pure effulgence being concealed under a coat of dust covering all the part of his body.

devanagari: ददर्श लोकान्विचरन्लोकतत्त्वविवित्सया । वृतोऽमात्यैः कतिपयैः प्रह्रादो भगवत्प्रियः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: dadarśa lokānvicaranlokatattvavivitsayāvṛto'mātyaiḥ katipayaiḥ prahrādo bhagavatpriyaḥ
translation: While Prahlāda, the beloved (devotee) of the Lord, accompanied by a few ministers, was touring through various worlds with a view to acquaint himself with the true nature of the people, he happened to see on the top of the Sahyādri (Western ghats) on the bank of the Kāverī, some person lying on bare ground, his pure effulgence being concealed under a coat of dust covering all the part of his body.

devanagari: कर्मणाकृतिभिर्वाचा लिङ्गैर्वर्णाश्रमादिभिः । न विदन्ति जना यं वै सोऽसाविति न वेति च ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: karmaṇākṛtibhirvācā liṅgairvarṇāśramādibhiḥna vidanti janā yaṃ vai so'sāviti na veti ca
translation: By his actions, appearances, words of external emblems indicative of his caste (varṇa) and stage in life (āśrama), people could not recognize him who or what he was and what he was not.

devanagari: तं नत्वाभ्यर्च्य विधिवत्पादयोः शिरसा स्पृशन् । विवित्सुरिदमप्राक्षीन्महाभागवतोऽसुरः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ natvābhyarcya vidhivatpādayoḥ śirasā spṛśanvivitsuridamaprākṣīnmahābhāgavato'suraḥ
translation: Having bowed the sage by touching his feet with his head and having worshipped him with due formalities, the Asura, (himself) a prominent votary of the Lord, who was curious to know (the truth about) him, (the sage) asked him the following question.

devanagari: बिभर्षि कायं पीवानं सोद्यमो भोगवान्यथा । वित्तं चैवोद्यमवतां भोगो वित्तवतामिह । भोगिनां खलु देहोऽयं पीवा भवति नान्यथा ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: bibharṣi kāyaṃ pīvānaṃ sodyamo bhogavānyathāvittaṃ caivodyamavatāṃ bhogo vittavatāmihabhogināṃ khalu deho'yaṃ pīvā bhavati nānyathā
translation: “You have a robust body like an industrious person who enjoys a luxurious life. Wealth accrues to persons who are industrious and comforts and luxuries can be afforded by moneyed people. Indeed only the bodies of those who are given to luxurious life become corpulent and not otherwise.

devanagari: न ते शयानस्य निरुद्यमस्य ब्रह्मन्नु हार्थो यत एव भोगः । अभोगिनोऽयं तव विप्र देहः पीवा यतस्तद्वद नः क्षमं चेत् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: na te śayānasya nirudyamasya brahmannu hārtho yata eva bhogaḥabhogino'yaṃ tava vipra dehaḥ pīvā yatastadvada naḥ kṣamaṃ cet
translation: Lying supine without doing any work as you do, Oh Brahman, you obviously possess no wealth which is the source of enjoyment of a luxurious life). If you think proper (to disclose), please tell us how your body is corpulent despite your non-indulgence in luxuries and lack of comforts.

devanagari: कविः कल्पो निपुणदृक्चित्रप्रियकथः समः । लोकस्य कुर्वतः कर्म शेषे तद्वीक्षितापि वा ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: kaviḥ kalpo nipuṇadṛkcitrapriyakathaḥ samaḥlokasya kurvataḥ karma śeṣe tadvīkṣitāpi vā
translation: Learned, capable, clever, possessing wonderfully sweet power of speech and of equable temper as you are, how is it that you lie down (doing nothing) simply looking on that other people are exerting themselves.”

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । स इत्थं दैत्यपतिना परिपृष्टो महामुनिः । स्मयमानस्तमभ्याह तद्वागमृतयन्त्रितः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācasa itthaṃ daityapatinā paripṛṣṭo mahāmuniḥsmayamānastamabhyāha tadvāgamṛtayantritaḥ
translation:
Nārada said:
Being thus questioned by Prahlāda, the king of Daityas, the great sage, being magnetized by his nectarlike speech, smilingly spoke to him.

devanagari: श्रीब्राह्मण उवाच । वेदेदमसुरश्रेष्ठ भवान्नन्वार्यसम्मतः । ईहोपरमयोर्नॄणां पदान्यध्यात्मचक्षुषा ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrībrāhmaṇa uvācavededamasuraśreṣṭha bhavānnanvāryasammataḥīhoparamayornṝṇāṃ padānyadhyātmacakṣuṣā
translation:
The Brāhmaṇa said:
Oh foremost of Asuras! Your honour is esteemed by all great and learned people. By your spiritual insight you certainly know this, viz. the consequences of activity for (obtaining) worldly objects and of renouncing them.

devanagari: यस्य नारायणो देवो भगवान्हृद्गतः सदा । भक्त्या केवलयाज्ञानं धुनोति ध्वान्तमर्कवत् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: yasya nārāyaṇo devo bhagavānhṛdgataḥ sadābhaktyā kevalayājñānaṃ dhunoti dhvāntamarkavat
translation: Due to your absolute and pure devotion, the glorious Lord Nārāyaṇa always abides in your heart. He dispels your ignorance like unto the Sun dispersing darkness.

devanagari: तथापि ब्रूमहे प्रश्नांस्तव राजन्यथाश्रुतम् । सम्भाषणीयो हि भवानात्मनः शुद्धिमिच्छता ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: tathāpi brūmahe praśnāṃstava rājanyathāśrutamsambhāṣaṇīyo hi bhavānātmanaḥ śuddhimicchatā
translation: We (I) however, shall (try to) answer your questions according to what I have traditionally learnt (about them). For you deserve respect from those who desire to purify their hearts.

devanagari: तृष्णया भववाहिन्या योग्यैः कामैरपूर्यया । कर्माणि कार्यमाणोऽहं नानायोनिषु योजितः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tṛṣṇayā bhavavāhinyā yogyaiḥ kāmairapūryayākarmāṇi kāryamāṇo'haṃ nānāyoniṣu yojitaḥ
translation: Being (induced) to do various actions by powerful desire which is never satiated by enjoyment of suitable objects, and thus becomes the source of the stream of births (and deaths), I was forced to take birth in various species.

devanagari: यदृच्छया लोकमिमं प्रापितः कर्मभिर्भ्रमन् । स्वर्गापवर्गयोर्द्वारं तिरश्चां पुनरस्य च ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: yadṛcchayā lokamimaṃ prāpitaḥ karmabhirbhramansvargāpavargayordvāraṃ tiraścāṃ punarasya ca
translation: While wandering (through various types of existence) by force of karmas, and through sheer providence, I was brought to the human form of existence which is a gate-way to heaven or final emancipation or to sub-human life or to human life again.

devanagari: तत्रापि दम्पतीनां च सुखायान्यापनुत्तये । कर्माणि कुर्वतां दृष्ट्वा निवृत्तोऽस्मि विपर्ययम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpi dampatīnāṃ ca sukhāyānyāpanuttayekarmāṇi kurvatāṃ dṛṣṭvā nivṛtto'smi viparyayam
translation: Having observed the frustrations and failures of married couples while performing actions for securing happiness and avoiding pain in this life as well, I retired from worldly-activities.

devanagari: सुखमस्यात्मनो रूपं सर्वेहोपरतिस्तनुः । मनःसंस्पर्शजान्दृष्ट्वा भोगान्स्वप्स्यामि संविशन् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sukhamasyātmano rūpaṃ sarvehoparatistanuḥmanaḥsaṃsparśajāndṛṣṭvā bhogānsvapsyāmi saṃviśan
translation: Happiness is the essential nature of the Soul[6]. It is manifested after withdrawal and cessation of all activities. Having perceived that enjoyments and experiences are fanciful creations of the mind I lie down (supine enjoying whatever is ordained for me by destiny).
purport:
Footnote [6]:
Padaratnāvalī thinks that the grace of the Lord is necessary for the realization of His bliss.

devanagari: इत्येतदात्मनः स्वार्थं सन्तं विस्मृत्य वै पुमान् । विचित्रामसति द्वैते घोरामाप्नोति संसृतिम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ityetadātmanaḥ svārthaṃ santaṃ vismṛtya vai pumānvicitrāmasati dvaite ghorāmāpnoti saṃsṛtim
translation: Having forgotten that this blissful nature is inherent in one’s self, a man is verily enmeshed into saṃsāra which is terrible (owing to birth, death and other miseries) and strange (as it consists of birth in celestial, subhuman, human and other-species).

devanagari: जलं तदुद्भवैश्छन्नं हित्वाज्ञो जलकाम्यया । मृगतृष्णामुपाधावेत्तथान्यत्रार्थदृक्स्वतः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: jalaṃ tadudbhavaiśchannaṃ hitvājño jalakāmyayāmṛgatṛṣṇāmupādhāvettathānyatrārthadṛksvataḥ
translation: He who (being ignorant of his innate blissful state) thinks of finding his object (happiness) elsewhere (outside his Self), is like an ignorant fellow who with a desire to get water (to quench his thirst) leaves water (near him but) covered with acquatic weeds and other overgrowth and runs after the mirage.

devanagari: देहादिभिर्दैवतन्त्रैरात्मनः सुखमीहतः । दुःखात्ययं चानीशस्य क्रिया मोघाः कृताः कृताः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: dehādibhirdaivatantrairātmanaḥ sukhamīhataḥduḥkhātyayaṃ cānīśasya kriyā moghāḥ kṛtāḥ kṛtāḥ
translation: The body and sense-organs are controlled by Destiny. All the actions and efforts repeatedly done through their instrumentality for securing his happiness and removing his miseries by a luckless person, become fruitlesss.

devanagari: आध्यात्मिकादिभिर्दुःखैरविमुक्तस्य कर्हिचित् । मर्त्यस्य कृच्छ्रोपनतैरर्थैः कामैः क्रियेत किम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: ādhyātmikādibhirduḥkhairavimuktasya karhicitmartyasya kṛcchropanatairarthaiḥ kāmaiḥ kriyeta kim
translation: (Granting that a man’s efforts are successful) what pleasure can be derived from the hard-earned wealth and desired objects obtained with great difficulty to a mortal (obsessed with the fear of death) and plagued by (lit. not immune from) three types of miseries such as bodily ailments and others.

devanagari: पश्यामि धनिनां क्लेशं लुब्धानामजितात्मनाम् । भयादलब्धनिद्रा णां सर्वतोऽभिविशङ्किनाम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: paśyāmi dhanināṃ kleśaṃ lubdhānāmajitātmanāmbhayādalabdhanidrā ṇāṃ sarvato'bhiviśaṅkinām
translation: I perceive the agonies and tensions of wealthy covetous persons who have no control over themselves, and who have lost their sleep, out of fear, as they are suspicious of everybody on all sides.

devanagari: राजतश्चौरतः शत्रोः स्वजनात्पशुपक्षितः । अर्थिभ्यः कालतः स्वस्मान्नित्यं प्राणार्थवद्भयम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: rājataścaurataḥ śatroḥ svajanātpaśupakṣitaḥarthibhyaḥ kālataḥ svasmānnityaṃ prāṇārthavadbhayam
translation: I observe that those who are anxious about their life and wealth, entertain fear from kings, robbers, enemies, their kinsmen, birds and beasts, beggers [beggars?], Time and themselves, at every moment.

devanagari: शोकमोहभयक्रोध रागक्लैब्यश्रमादयः । यन्मूलाः स्युर्नृणां जह्यात्स्पृहां प्राणार्थयोर्बुधः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: śokamohabhayakrodha rāgaklaibyaśramādayaḥyanmūlāḥ syurnṛṇāṃ jahyātspṛhāṃ prāṇārthayorbudhaḥ
translation: (Therefore) a wise man should give up longings for life and property which are the source of sorrow, infatuation, fear, anger, attachment, despondency, over-exertions and such other (troubles).

devanagari: मधुकारमहासर्पौ लोकेऽस्मिन्नो गुरूत्तमौ । वैराग्यं परितोषं च प्राप्ता यच्छिक्षया वयम् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: madhukāramahāsarpau loke'sminno gurūttamauvairāgyaṃ paritoṣaṃ ca prāptā yacchikṣayā vayam
translation: In this world the bee and the big python are our best teachers, for following their example we have attained renunciation and contentment.

devanagari: विरागः सर्वकामेभ्यः शिक्षितो मे मधुव्रतात् । कृच्छ्राप्तं मधुवद्वित्तं हत्वाप्यन्यो हरेत्पतिम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: virāgaḥ sarvakāmebhyaḥ śikṣito me madhuvratātkṛcchrāptaṃ madhuvadvittaṃ hatvāpyanyo haretpatim
translation: I have learnt renunciation of all worldly objects from the bee, the gatherer of honey, for any other person may kill the lord and usurp the hard-earned money like honey after killing the bees.

devanagari: अनीहः परितुष्टात्मा यदृच्छोपनतादहम् । नो चेच्छये बह्वहानि महाहिरिव सत्त्ववान् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: anīhaḥ parituṣṭātmā yadṛcchopanatādahamno cecchaye bahvahāni mahāhiriva sattvavān
translation: Being free from all desires and with contented mind, I accept what is brought to me by providence. If not, I lie inactive like a big serpent for many days, depending on my power.

devanagari: क्वचिदल्पं क्वचिद्भूरि भुञ्जेऽन्नं स्वाद्वस्वादु वा । क्वचिद्भूरि गुणोपेतं गुणहीनमुत क्वचित् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacidalpaṃ kvacidbhūri bhuñje'nnaṃ svādvasvādu vākvacidbhūri guṇopetaṃ guṇahīnamuta kvacit
translation: Sometimes I eat scant food; sometimes I enjoy a heavy meal irrespective of the sweetness or otherwise of the food. Sometime I partake of highly rich and dainty dishes and sometimes worthless food.

devanagari: श्रद्धयोपहृतं क्वापि कदाचिन्मानवर्जितम् । भुञ्जे भुक्त्वाथ कस्मिंश्चिद्दिवा नक्तं यदृच्छया ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: śraddhayopahṛtaṃ kvāpi kadācinmānavarjitambhuñje bhuktvātha kasmiṃściddivā naktaṃ yadṛcchayā
translation: At some places I eat food, that is offered to me with respect and sometimes with irreverence. Sometimes I do justice to food after eating; sometimes I eat food by day or by night as it is offered to me by chance.

devanagari: क्षौमं दुकूलमजिनं चीरं वल्कलमेव वा । वसेऽन्यदपि सम्प्राप्तं दिष्टभुक्तुष्टधीरहम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣaumaṃ dukūlamajinaṃ cīraṃ valkalameva vāvase'nyadapi samprāptaṃ diṣṭabhuktuṣṭadhīraham
translation: Remaining contented in mind, I enjoy what is ordained by fate, and I wear linen garments or silks or deerskin or rags or any other (fabric) that is offered to me.

devanagari: क्वचिच्छये धरोपस्थे तृणपर्णाश्मभस्मसु । क्वचित्प्रासादपर्यङ्के कशिपौ वा परेच्छया ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: kvacicchaye dharopasthe tṛṇaparṇāśmabhasmasukvacitprāsādaparyaṅke kaśipau vā parecchayā
translation: Sometimes I sleep on the bare ground; sometimes on grass or on a heap of leaves or on a slab of stone or in ashes; sometimes I lie inside a mansion on a rich bed over a precious bedstead as desired by God (or other people).

devanagari: क्वचित्स्नातोऽनुलिप्ताङ्गः सुवासाः स्रग्व्यलङ्कृतः । रथेभाश्वैश्चरे क्वापि दिग्वासा ग्रहवद्विभो ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: kvacitsnāto'nuliptāṅgaḥ suvāsāḥ sragvyalaṅkṛtaḥrathebhāśvaiścare kvāpi digvāsā grahavadvibho
translation: Sometimes I take bath with my body anointed with fragrant pigments. I put on rich garments and wear garlands and ornaments. Sometimes I ride in a chariot or on an elephant or on a horse and sometimes I wander stark-naked like an evil spirit, Oh king.

devanagari: नाहं निन्दे न च स्तौमि स्वभावविषमं जनम् । एतेषां श्रेय आशासे उतैकात्म्यं महात्मनि ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: nāhaṃ ninde na ca staumi svabhāvaviṣamaṃ janameteṣāṃ śreya āśāse utaikātmyaṃ mahātmani
translation: I neither revile nor praise people who are of diverse nature. I pray for their welfare and bless them with their union with the Almighty Lord Viṣṇu.

devanagari: विकल्पं जुहुयाच्चित्तौ तां मनस्यर्थविभ्रमे । मनो वैकारिके हुत्वा तं मायायां जुहोत्यनु ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: vikalpaṃ juhuyāccittau tāṃ manasyarthavibhramemano vaikārike hutvā taṃ māyāyāṃ juhotyanu
translation: One should oblate (merge) one’s notion of diversity in the mental faculty that perceives such differences, that mental faculty into mind which mistakes the unreal for the real, the mind into the sāttvic ahaṃkāra and should absorb that ahaṃkāra through mahat in the Māyā regularly.

devanagari: आत्मानुभूतौ तां मायां जुहुयात्सत्यदृङ्मुनिः । ततो निरीहो विरमेत्स्वानुभूत्यात्मनि स्थितः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānubhūtau tāṃ māyāṃ juhuyātsatyadṛṅmuniḥtato nirīho virametsvānubhūtyātmani sthitaḥ
translation: The sage who perceives the Reality, should merge that Māyā into the realization of his soul. Being devoid of all desires, he should establish himself in self-realization and cease all activities.

devanagari: स्वात्मवृत्तं मयेत्थं ते सुगुप्तमपि वर्णितम् । व्यपेतं लोकशास्त्राभ्यां भवान्हि भगवत्परः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: svātmavṛttaṃ mayetthaṃ te suguptamapi varṇitamvyapetaṃ lokaśāstrābhyāṃ bhavānhi bhagavatparaḥ
translation: In this way I have described to you my way of life which is closely guarded as a secret, though it is far different from secular and Śāstric canons of behaviour. (I have thus frankly explained this to you) as you are the beloved of God.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । धर्मं पारमहंस्यं वै मुनेः श्रुत्वासुरेश्वरः । पूजयित्वा ततः प्रीत आमन्त्र्य प्रययौ गृहम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācadharmaṃ pāramahaṃsyaṃ vai muneḥ śrutvāsureśvaraḥpūjayitvā tataḥ prīta āmantrya prayayau gṛham
translation:
Nārada said:
Having heard the code of conduct of the highest order of recluses, the Lord of Asuras (Prahlāda) worshipped him respectfully. Being pleased in mind he bade goodbye, and returned home.
[back to top]
